image:image.image:title: EVing #1: How to make simple EV spreads - Make it stand out
image:image.image:caption: Whatever it is, the way you tell your story online can make all the difference.

image:image.image:title: Introduction to Competitive Pokemon - Make it stand out
image:image.image:caption: This is a picture of team preview. The player has selected four of their Pokemon to come to battle against their opponent's team (left). The Pokemon will be sent out in the order that they're selected (so Kyogre and Tornadus will be sent out immediately, while Xerneas and Rillaboom wait in the back).

image:image.image:title: Introduction to Competitive Pokemon - Make it stand out
image:image.image:caption: Pokemon Showdown is a popular fan-made site for playing battles over the internet (that is not affiliated with the Pokemon company). You don't even have to catch your own Pokemon– you can manually adjust the stats of your Pokemon.

image:image.image:title: Introduction to Competitive Pokemon - Make it stand out
image:image.image:caption: Colosseum was the first game that centered solely on Double Battles.

image:image.image:title: Introduction to Competitive Pokemon - Make it stand out
image:image.image:caption: Whatever it is, the way you tell your story online can make all the difference.

image:image.image:title: Introduction to Competitive Pokemon - Make it stand out
image:image.image:caption: Raihan's gym in Pokemon Sword and Shield featured several different double battles, and the trainers had Pokemon that made use of each other's abilities.

image:image.image:title: Introduction to Competitive Pokemon - Make it stand out
image:image.image:caption: This is called the “Battle Box”. It’s where you arrange Pokemon into teams to battle with in-game. You can access it by pressing X twice when you’re in the PC in Pokemon Sword and Shield.

image:image.image:title: Context Pt1 - Make it stand out

image:image.image:title: Items - Make it stand out
image:image.image:caption: Whatever it is, the way you tell your story online can make all the difference.

image:image.image:title: About Us - Aaron Traylor author
image:image.image:caption: I’ve been playing VGC since 2011, and I’ve been writing about it for almost as long. I believe that the friendship between Trainers and their Pokémon is ultimately what leads to success in battle. Here are my best results: 2011 San Jose Regional Champion (Senior division) 2011 US Nationals Top 4 (Senior division) 2015 St. Louis Regional Champion 2016 US Nationals 2nd place 2016 World Championships Top 8 2019 World Championships Top 16 2020 Dallas Regional Champion Outside of Pokemon, I work as a PhD student in computer science and cognitive science. I’m not really a content creator or a YouTuber! Follow me on Twitter or you can read some of my other writing here. You can watch some of my Pokemon battles here: 2020 Dallas Regionals, 2016 World Championships, 2016 US National Championships, 2015 St. Louis Regionals. I am also very interested in Pokemon AI. If you want to read some of my (old) thoughts, check them out here. Thank you for taking the time to read our guide!

image:image.image:title: About Us - Scarlet author
image:image.image:caption: I’ve been playing VGC casually since 2014, but have been competing seriously since the release of Sword and Shield. I’m a big fan of Marks and Ribbons, and making fun memories with your Pokemon! My favorite Pokemon is Mienshao. I helped put together the in-game resources guide with Max, and I’m really excited to help more people get into the wonderful game of VGC! Outside Pokemon I’m a graphic designer and cosplayer, and play bass in my spare time. You can follow me on twitter here.

image:image.image:title: About Us - Max author
image:image.image:caption: I’ve been playing Pokemon games since 2004 when I started with LeafGreen. I started getting into VGC in late 2019 with the release of Sword and Shield, and I helped put together the in-game resources guide with Scarlet. Outside of Pokemon, I work as a journalist and watch a lot of baseball.

image:image.image:title: About Us - Aaron Zheng author
image:image.image:caption: I’ve been a competitive VGC player since 2008, a content creator on YouTube since 2014, and a commentator for The Pokémon Company International since 2016! Here are some of major competitive results: 5x Regional Champion (2011, 2013, 2014, 2016, 2017) 2x National Champion (2011, 2012) 2013 Worlds Top 4 2012 Worlds Top 8 8x Worlds Qualifier (2008, 2011-2017) I’ve also casted the following events: Nationals / Internationals: US 2016, Oceania 2018, NA 2018, LATAM 2019, NA 2019, Oceania 2020 Worlds: 2016, 2018, 2019 Players Cups: 1, 3, 25th Invitational I studied economics & applied mathematics in undergrad at Claremont McKenna & Columbia University. I’ll be an MBA candidate at Stanford Graduate School of Business this upcoming fall, and I’m really interested in the intersection of business & gaming/esports. You can find me on Twitter & YouTube. Thank you for reading our guide!

image:image.image:title: About Us - Marissa L UX/UI Developer
image:image.image:caption: I’ve always been interested in VGC since learning about it in 2012, but have only been casually competing since 2019 with Sword and Shield. I designed and built the site you are currently on! In learning competitive play I’ve become interested in helping others improve and understand the game on a deeper level. I am professionally a UX/UI engineer and thankful I get to use my skills to help build a resource like this for the community. I’m kind of funny on the internet so feel free to follow me on Twitter or Twitch.

image:image.image:title: About Us - Wolfe Glick author
image:image.image:caption: I’ve been playing VGC since 2011, and making content on it since 2016. My goal is to be the greatest player of all time. Here are my major results: 6x Regional Champion (2011, 2015, 2015, 2015, 2016, 2018) 3x US National Champion (2011, 2012, 2019) Players Cup 2 Champion (2021) World Champion (2016) Competed in every World Championship since 2011 4x Worlds Top Cut (2011 top 8, 2012 2nd, 2016 1st, 2017 top 16) 6x Worlds Top 16 (11, 12, 14, 15, 16, 17) Check out my Twitter here and my YouTube Channel here. You can watch some of my battles here: 2016 Worlds Finals, 2019 US Nationals Finals, Players Cup 2 Grand Finals I hope you enjoy our guide!
